DO.action: Increase aeration or water exchange. Keep DO above 4.5 mg/L to prevent stress.
DO.critical_action: CRITICAL: Turn on maximum aeration immediately — hypoxia risk. Skip feeding until DO recovers above 4.5 mg/L.
DO.source: Ott et al. 2024; ScienceDirect aeration study 2022

turbidity.action: Do a 20-30% partial water change and reduce feeding. Overfeeding is the main waste source.
turbidity.critical_action: CRITICAL: Perform 50% water change now. Suspend feeding for 24h. Check filter and aeration system.
turbidity.source: Yu et al. 2018; Ramli et al. 2024

pH.action: If low: add alkaline buffer. If high: partial water change. Target pH 7.0-8.5.
pH.critical_action: CRITICAL: Immediate action needed — pH shock risk. Perform 30-40% water change and test source water. Add buffer if pH < 6.5.
pH.source: UF/IFAS Water Quality Guide; Zeng et al. 2024

temp.action: Add shade cover, increase water exchange, boost aeration (warm water holds less oxygen). Target 24-30 °C.
temp.critical_action: CRITICAL: Reduce temperature immediately — increase water exchange rate to max. Add ice packs if needed. Monitor DO closely.

waterLevel.action: Top up or drain to restore normal level. Low level concentrates waste and raises stocking stress.
waterLevel.critical_action: CRITICAL: Address water level immediately. Extremely low level risks mass mortality. Check pump and inlet before restoring.
